agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is proposed to show customers their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator, while Operations demands location never be shown due to driver retention losses from surveillance concerns.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Courier Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A delivery driver employed or contracted by the company to fulfill last-mile delivery of packages to customers."},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"The courier's GPS-derived position, updated on a frequent interval, during an active delivery."},{"term":"Delivery window","definition":"The time period between a delivery being dispatched and its completion (delivered or failed)."},{"term":"Customer","definition":"The end recipient of a package who has a vested interest in knowing when and how their delivery will arrive."},{"term":"Driver retention","definition":"The company's ability to keep couriers on the platform; a key operational metric directly tied to delivery capacity and cost."},{"term":"ETA","definition":"Estimated Time of Arrival — a derived, privacy-preserving proxy for location that communicates delivery progress without exposing exact coordinates."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for extended period (e.g., at a restaurant, stuck in traffic)","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — depends on whether location or abstracted progress is shown. Exact GPS would expose the courier waiting; an ETA/progress model could gracefully handle this."},{"scenario":"Courier deviates from expected route (personal errand, multi-drop optimization)","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — exact GPS exposes potentially legitimate deviations that alarm customers. Abstracted tracking avoids this. Resolution depends on which stakeholder requirement prevails."},{"scenario":"Courier is in a sensitive location (hospital, domestic violence shelter)","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — exact GPS raises serious safety and privacy concerns. This edge case strengthens Operations' position but must be weighed against Customer Success goals."},{"scenario":"Customer repeatedly refreshes or screenshots courier location","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — harassment/stalking vector if exact GPS is shown. This is a safety invariant that may have legal implications."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"UNRESOLVABLE WITHOUT EXECUTIVE DECISION. Showing exact GPS may increase customer conversion and reduce 'where is my order' support calls (Customer Success claims). Hiding location entirely may reduce driver churn and recruitment costs (Operations claims). Both have direct P&L impact. A wrong call in either direction damages revenue (lost customers) or supply (lost drivers). The two-sided marketplace cannot function without both.","operational":"Driver retention is an existential operational concern — without couriers, there is no delivery service. Customer visibility is a competitive concern — without differentiation, growth stalls. These are in direct tension and represent a classic two-sided marketplace balancing act.","user":"Customers want delivery predictability. Couriers want privacy and dignity. Both are 'users' of the platform. The spec as written forces a choice that harms one group entirely.","risk":"HIGH. Legal/privacy risk if exact GPS is shown without courier consent (GDPR, labor law depending on jurisdiction). Retention risk if drivers leave. Competitive risk if customers see no tracking at all. Reputational risk in either direction.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Directly affected — their delivery experience depends on the level of visibility provided."},{"group":"Couriers/Drivers","impact":"Directly affected — their privacy, safety, and sense of workplace dignity are at stake. They are also a scarce resource the business depends on."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Their KPIs (CSAT, NPS, support ticket volume) are tied to customer-facing transparency features."},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Their KPIs (driver retention, fulfillment capacity, recruitment cost) are tied to driver satisfaction."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"NOT YET CONSULTED — exact GPS sharing of worker location to third parties has regulatory implications (GDPR Article 6, labor surveillance laws) that neither team has addressed."},{"group":"Executive leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate this conflict — it is a strategic product direction decision, not a feature spec decision."}]},"confidence":{"level":35,"reasoning":"Confidence is critically low because the specification contains a direct, unresolvable contradiction between two non-negotiable requirements from equally authoritative stakeholder teams. Neither team has the organizational authority to overrule the other based on the information provided. Additionally, Legal/Compliance has not been consulted on what may be a regulated decision. No acceptance criteria can be safely defined until the contradiction is resolved at a level above both teams.","high_confidence_areas":["This IS a genuine contradiction — the two requirements are mutually exclusive as stated","Both stakeholder concerns are legitimate and grounded in real business impact","A middle-ground solution likely exists (e.g., abstracted progress/ETA instead of exact GPS) but neither team has been asked about acceptable compromises","Legal review is mandatory before any implementation"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which business priority takes precedence (growth vs. supply retention)","Whether either team would accept a compromise (e.g., zone-level tracking, ETA-only, opt-in by couriers)","Jurisdictional legal requirements for sharing worker GPS with customers","Whether driver retention losses are causally linked to tracking or merely correlated"],"assumptions":["Both teams have equal organizational authority to set requirements","No executive decision has already been made that supersedes either team","The company operates in jurisdictions where worker location sharing has legal implications"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS coordinates be shown to customers at all times during delivery. Operations requires courier location NEVER be shown to customers. These are mutually exclusive, non-negotiable requirements from two peer-level stakeholder teams. This cannot be resolved at the feature-spec level — it is a strategic product direction decision that requires executive arbitration.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers","Customer Success team","Operations team","Legal/Compliance","Executive leadership"],"options":["A) Side with Customer Success: show exact GPS. Risk: accelerated driver churn, potential labor/privacy legal exposure.","B) Side with Operations: show no location. Risk: competitive disadvantage, higher support costs, lower customer satisfaction.","C) Seek compromise: show abstracted progress (ETA, zone-level indicator, delivery stage) instead of exact GPS. Preserves customer predictability without exposing courier coordinates. Requires BOTH teams to accept a middle ground.","D) Make courier tracking opt-in for drivers with appropriate incentives, letting drivers choose their privacy level."],"recommendation":"Option C — abstracted progress tracking (ETA + delivery stage like 'Nearby' / 'Arriving') is the industry-standard solution to this exact tension. It gives customers the predictability they need without the surveillance couriers object to. However, I CANNOT define acceptance criteria for this or any option until an executive decision is made. Recommend convening Customer Success, Operations, Legal, and a product executive to align on approach before any spec work proceeds."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Legal/Compliance has not been consulted. Sharing a worker's exact GPS coordinates with a third party (the customer) may violate GDPR, local labor surveillance laws, or employment contracts. This is a potential regulatory blocker that could make Option A illegal regardless of business preference.","affected_stakeholders":["Legal/Compliance","Couriers","Executive leadership"],"options":["Engage Legal/Compliance review before proceeding","Proceed without legal review (NOT recommended)"],"recommendation":"Mandatory Legal/Compliance review before any implementation decision. Do not write code until this is resolved."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Contradictory requirements: 'must show exact GPS at all times' vs 'must never show location'","business_rule_broken":"No consistent business rule can be derived — the two requirements are mutually exclusive as stated."},{"violation":"Missing Legal/Compliance review for worker location sharing","business_rule_broken":"Privacy and labor law compliance is a hard invariant for any feature involving personal location data of employees/contractors."}]}
